title: mentha × verticillata var. paludosa
intro: Mentha × verticillata var. paludosa, also known as Marsh Peppermint, is a variety of the water mint plant, characterized by its square stems and whorled leaves. The medicinal properties of Marsh Peppermint include its ability to act as an anti-inflammatory and antiseptic, making it effective in treating digestive issues, headaches, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Marsh Peppermint include its potential to alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. Marsh Peppermint can be consumed in various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the leaves in hot water or combining them with other herbs to create a remedy for a range of health issues.
